diff_user.system:
You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its score and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Airport. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

# Current Program Information

# Current Program
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about airports (or related entities) into a single
    fluent sentence. Unknown predicates fall back to a generic phrasing.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    def _clean(val: str) -> str:
        """Remove surrounding quotes if present."""
        return val.strip('"').strip("'")

    parts: list[str] = []
    for t in triples:
        s = t.subject
        p = t.predicate
        o = _clean(t.object)

        # Specific phrasing for common predicates
        if p == "cityServed":
            parts.append(f"{s} serves the city of {o}")
        elif p == "location":
            parts.append(f"{s} is located in {o}")
        elif p == "country":
            parts.append(f"{s} is in {o}")
        elif p == "isPartOf":
            parts.append(f"{s} is part of {o}")
        elif p == "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel":
            parts.append(f"{s} lies at an elevation of {o} metres above sea level")
        elif p == "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet":
            parts.append(f"{s} lies at an elevation of {o} feet above sea level")
        elif p == "operatingOrganisation":
            parts.append(f"{s} is operated by {o}")
        elif p == "runwaySurfaceType":
            parts.append(f"{s} has a runway surface of {o}")
        elif p.endswith("RunwayLengthFeet"):
            # e.g., 1stRunwayLengthFeet -> first runway
            idx = p.split("Runway")[0]
            parts.append(f"{s}'s {idx.lower()} runway is {o} feet long")
        elif p.endswith("RunwayLengthMetre"):
            idx = p.split("Runway")[0]
            parts.append(f"{s}'s {idx.lower()} runway is {o} metres long")
        elif p == "runwayName":
            parts.append(f"{s}'s runway is named {o}")
        elif p == "runwayLength":
            parts.append(f"{s}'s runway length is {o}")
        else:
            # Generic fallback
            parts.append(f"{s} has {p} {o}")

    # Join parts into one sentence
    sentence = ", ".join(parts) + "."
    # Capitalise first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```

# Task
Suggest an improvement to the current program that will improve the combined_score.
Different solutions with similar combined_score score but different ideas are valuable.
Creating one complex sentence from all the given triples will award greater score as well.

=======
def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about airports (or related entities) into a single
    fluent sentence. Unknown predicates fall back to a generic phrasing.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    def _clean(val: str) -> str:
        """Remove surrounding quotes if present."""
        return val.strip('"').strip("'")

    def _ordinal(n: str) -> str:
        """Convert ordinal prefix to English word."""
        return {"1st": "first", "2nd": "second", "3rd": "third", "4th": "fourth", "5th": "fifth"}.get(n, n.lower())

    # Group predicates by category for better sentence structure
    location_facts = []
    runway_facts = []
    operational_facts = []
    identifier_facts = []
    other_facts = []

    subject = triples[0].subject
    for t in triples:
        s = t.subject
        p = t.predicate
        o = _clean(t.object)

        if p == "cityServed":
            location_facts.append(f"serves the city of {o}")
        elif p == "location":
            location_facts.append(f"located in {o}")
        elif p == "country":
            location_facts.append(f"in {o}")
        elif p == "isPartOf":
            location_facts.append(f"part of {o}")
        elif p == "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel":
            location_facts.append(f"at an elevation of {o} metres above sea level")
        elif p == "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet":
            location_facts.append(f"at an elevation of {o} feet above sea level")
        elif p == "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres":
            location_facts.append(f"at an elevation of {o} metres above sea level")
        elif p.endswith("RunwayLengthFeet"):
            idx = _ordinal(p.split("Runway")[0])
            runway_facts.append(f"{idx} runway is {o} feet long")
        elif p.endswith("RunwayLengthMetre"):
            idx = _ordinal(p.split("Runway")[0])
            runway_facts.append(f"{idx} runway is {o} metres long")
        elif p == "runwayLength":
            runway_facts.append(f"runway length of {o}")
        elif p.endswith("RunwaySurfaceType") or p == "runwaySurfaceType":
            runway_facts.append(f"{o} surface")
        elif p == "runwayName":
            runway_facts.append(f"named {o}")
        elif p == "operatingOrganisation":
            operational_facts.append(f"operated by {o}")
        elif p == "owner":
            operational_facts.append(f"owned by {o}")
        elif p == "icaoLocationIdentifier":
            identifier_facts.append(f"ICAO code {o}")
        elif p == "iataLocationIdentifier":
            identifier_facts.append(f"IATA code {o}")
        elif p == "locationIdentifier":
            identifier_facts.append(f"code {o}")
        else:
            other_facts.append(f"{p.replace('_', ' ')} of {o}")

    # Build coherent sentence with proper connectors
    parts = [subject]

    if location_facts:
        parts.append(", ".join(location_facts))

    if runway_facts:
        runway_text = " with " + ", ".join(runway_facts)
        parts.append(runway_text)

    if operational_facts:
        parts.append(" and " + ", ".join(operational_facts))

    if identifier_facts:
        parts.append(", with " + ", ".join(identifier_facts))

    if other_facts:
        parts.append(", including " + ", ".join(other_facts))

    sentence = ", ".join(parts) + "."
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
>>>>>>> REPLACE
